Mtn For Order Staying Depo & Quashing Ntc That Depo & For Protective Order Restricting Disco
Off Calendar Dec-02-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Samuel Bart for plaintiff and opposing party: United Services Automobile
COURT REPORTER: Diana Chen CSR#11312

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
The parties may appear by telephone. Defendant's motion to quash deposition notice and for a protective order is denied in part and granted in part. The motion to stay the deposition of defendant and to quash the deposition notice is denied. The motion for a protective order is granted in part. To the extent that requests #1 and #2 in plaintiff's notice of deposition may be interpreted as seeking production of medical and property damage claims without limitation, the Court orders that plaintiff produce the requested writings that relate to the incident that is the subject of the instant action. The Court further orders that the parties meet and confer as to a mutually convenient time to conduct the deposition of defendant. The deposition shall take place in a session, or sessions, not to exceed three hours per day. The deposition shall commence at a time mutually convenient to the parties and be completed by February 15, 2006. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Plaintiff'S Motion To Lift Judge Garcia'S Stay Re Usaa'S Relief From Stay In Bankruptcy Court
Off Calendar Sep-10-2004 Continued To Nov-30-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 304
MINI MINUTES: TELEPHONIC APPEARANCES:
Ian Fraser Thompson, Esq. and Samuel Bort, Esq. for Plaintiff
Defendant PATRICIA McCOLM in propria persona
2:25 p.m. This hearing was specially set by the Court this morning. Both parties were directed to appear telephonically by the Court. Ms. McColm submitted a Declaration of Robert R. Domush, M.D. attesting to her unavailability to adequately contest Plaintiff's motion to lift a stay previously imposed by the Hon. DAVID A. GARCIA. The Court having reviewed Dr. DOMUSH's declaration found that there is justification for Ms. McColm's unavailability to properly oppose Plaintiff's motion. Therefore, Plaintiff's motion is hereby restored on calendar for hearing on NOVEMBER 30, 2004 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 304. The Court granted leave to Ms. McColm to file supplemental opposition. (Hon. PAUL H. ALVARADO) Mitch Lyon, CSR 6840
